description:
This conceptual diagram illustrates the transformation of a geospatial dataset from a multipart feature representation to a singlepart feature representation.
The 'INPUT' state shows one "1 Multipart Feature", which consists of four spatially separate, irregularly shaped green polygons. Each of these four polygons is labelled with the number "1", indicating they are all parts of the same single logical feature.
An arrow points from the 'INPUT' to the 'OUTPUT' state, signifying a process of feature splitting.
The 'OUTPUT' state shows "4 Singlepart Features". These are four distinct, irregularly shaped polygons, each assigned a unique colour and label: a light purple polygon labelled "1", a yellow polygon labelled "2", a darker purple polygon labelled "3", and a light blue polygon labelled "4". This indicates that the original multipart feature has been successfully split into four independent singlepart features.
